message:
feat(tantivy_ingest): Cx-first run_with_reader (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Expand the Cx-first surface into the tantivy ingest indexer.
`IncrementalIndexer::run_with_reader` is a batch loop over a cursor
that reads recorder events, indexes them through the tantivy
writer, commits the index batch, and commits a per-batch checkpoint
to storage. Without cx threading, a long-running indexer run
ignores caller cancellation.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `IncrementalIndexer::run_with_reader_with_cx(cx, storage, reader)`
    — Cx-first parallel of `run_with_reader`. Adds:
      - `cx.checkpoint()?` at the top (pre-cancelled cx returns
        before cursor is opened).
      - `cx.checkpoint()?` at the start of each batch iteration,
        embedding `final_ordinal` in the cancellation error message
        so the caller knows where to resume.

Cancellation-safety contract: the batch iteration body is
structured (1) next_batch -> (2) index each record -> (3)
writer.commit -> (4) storage.commit_checkpoint -> (5) loop back to
top checkpoint. Because the storage checkpoint commit is INSIDE the
loop body (step 4), and the cx.checkpoint that can fire is at the
TOP of the next iteration (step 5), a mid-run cancel leaves the
last-committed checkpoint always consistent with the last fully-
indexed batch. No indexed-but-not-checkpointed state is leaked.

The legacy `run_with_reader` remains untouched. All existing
callers continue to use the ambient path.

Regression test (new) `run_with_reader_with_cx_matches_legacy`:

  * Populates an AppendLogRecorderStorage with 4 sample events.
  * Runs BOTH legacy `run_with_reader` and Cx-first
    `run_with_reader_with_cx` on separate consumer_ids (so their
    checkpoints don't collide).
  * Asserts IndexerRunResult parity across every field:
    events_read, events_indexed, events_skipped, caught_up,
    final_ordinal, batches_committed.
  * Sanity check that events_indexed > 0 so the test isn't
    vacuously equal on two empty results.

If a future refactor of `run_with_reader_with_cx` dropped a commit,
skipped a record-schema check, or changed the
is_final_batch / caught_up calculation, the parity assertions
would catch it even without firing a real cancellation.

Follow-ons:
  * `run_with_cx` — parallel for the `run` (AppendLog only) entry
    point. Same pattern.
  * `compute_indexer_lag_with_cx` — Cx-first lag snapshot.
  * Upgrade the checkpoints to thread cx *into*
    `storage.commit_checkpoint` once RecorderStorage gains
    `_with_cx` variants.

Verification (local fork bypass, CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):
  * tantivy_ingest:: — 121 passed / 0 failed (tick 51: 120 -> tick
    52: 121, +1 new parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (37.91s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
